claims.claim: Read is a prerequisite for all other page permissions except Display — granting other permissions without Read has no effect.
claims.location: Permission descriptions table — Read row (modified)
claims.evidence: WebPageAclPermissionEvaluator.cs lines 118-123: `if (WebsiteConstants.IndividuallyCheckedPermissions.Contains(permission)) { return grantedPermissions[webPageItemId].Contains(permission); } return grantedPermissions[webPageItemId].Contains(WebPageAclPermissions.READ) && grantedPermissions[webPageItemId].Contains(permission);` — IndividuallyCheckedPermissions is {Read, Display} per WebsiteConstants.cs lines 20-24. All other permissions require BOTH Read AND the specific permission.
claims.docFact: WebPageAclPermissionEvaluator.cs: All permissions except Read and Display require BOTH Read AND the specific permission

claims.claim: Six page-level ACL permissions: Display, Read, Create, Update, Delete, Synchronize
claims.location: Permission descriptions table (pre-existing, verified for context)
claims.evidence: WebPageAclPermissions.cs lines 11-42: DISPLAY, READ, CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, SYNCHRONIZE — exactly six constants defined.
claims.docFact: WebPageAclPermissions.cs: Six page-level ACL permissions (Read, Update, Create, Delete, Synchronize, Display)

claims.claim: When deleting a page that has child pages, the system also checks the Delete permission on child pages that have broken the permission inheritance.
claims.location: Permission descriptions table — Delete row (modified), also repeated in 'Page permissions for deleting pages' section
claims.evidence: WebPagesApplication.cs lines 528 and 551: `await CheckAclPermission(args.WebPageItemId, WebPageAclPermissions.DELETE, true, ...)` — the `true` parameter is `evaluateChildPermissions`. WebPageAclPermissionEvaluator.cs line 91-93: when evaluateChildPages=true, calls CheckAclSubtreePermission. Line 130 remark: 'For performance purposes, only web pages with broken inheritance are checked.' — InnerJoin with WebPageAclInfo (broken inheritance marker) at line 146.
claims.docFact: permissionSummary.deletePage: VIEW + Read + Delete ACL. evaluateChildPages=true

claims.claim: The Read permission is a prerequisite for all page permissions except Display. If you grant Update, Create, Delete, or Synchronize without also granting Read, the granted permission has no effect.
claims.location: Warning callout (added after permission descriptions table)
claims.evidence: Same as claim 1 — WebPageAclPermissionEvaluator.cs lines 118-123. IndividuallyCheckedPermissions = {Read, Display} per WebsiteConstants.cs. For any permission not in that set, the evaluator requires BOTH Read AND the specific permission in grantedPermissions.
claims.docFact: WebPageAclPermissionEvaluator.cs: All permissions except Read and Display require BOTH Read AND the specific permission

claims.claim: Without Read, users cannot access page content in the administration.
claims.location: Warning callout (added after permission descriptions table)
claims.evidence: WebPageBase.cs lines 132-134: `ConfigurePage()` calls `await CheckAclPermission(WebPageIdentifier.WebPageItemID, WebPageAclPermissions.READ);` — this runs on every web page tab load. Without Read, ForbiddenAccessException is thrown at lines 194-200.
claims.docFact: WebPageBase.cs: ConfigurePage() enforces Read ACL on every web page tab

claims.claim: The Administrator role and roles with the Manage permissions permission bypass all page permission checks and always have full access to all pages in a channel.
claims.location: Channel admin tip (modified in 'Allow users to work with website channel content' section)
claims.evidence: WebsiteChannelAdminProvider.cs lines 43-52: `IsChannelAdmin()` checks (1) `user.IsAdministrator()` → returns true, and (2) `GetManagingWebsiteChannels()` which queries for MANAGE_PERMISSIONS permission (line 77). WebPageAclPermissionEvaluator.cs lines 83-87: if `userIsChannelAdmin` is true, returns `WebPageAclPermissionEvaluationResult.Success` immediately — bypassing ALL ACL checks.
claims.docFact: WebsiteChannelAdminProvider.cs: Channel admins bypass all ACL checks. Two paths: (1) user.IsAdministrator() — global Administrator role, (2) user has ManagePermissions for the website channel application. Cache TTL is 60 minutes.

claims.claim: The system caches this status for up to 60 minutes, so changes to these role assignments may not take effect immediately.
claims.location: Channel admin tip (modified in 'Allow users to work with website channel content' section)
claims.evidence: WebsiteChannelAdminProvider.cs line 26: `private const int HOUR_CACHE_DURATION = 60;` — used at line 65 in `CacheSettings(HOUR_CACHE_DURATION, true, ...)`. The CacheSettings constructor first parameter is cache duration in minutes. 60 minutes = up to 1 hour cache.
claims.docFact: WebsiteChannelAdminProvider.cs: Channel admins bypass all ACL checks. Two paths: (1) user.IsAdministrator() — global Administrator role, (2) user has ManagePermissions for the website channel application. Cache TTL is 60 minutes.

claims.claim: To perform an operation on a page, a user typically needs: Application permission — the Access channel permission for the website channel application.
claims.location: Permission requirements for page operations section (added)
claims.evidence: All page commands across ContentTab.cs and CreateWebPage.cs use `[PageCommand(Permission = SystemPermissions.VIEW)]` or equivalent, and the WebPagesApplication class at line 38 has `[UIEvaluatePermission(SystemPermissions.VIEW)]`. SystemPermissions.VIEW corresponds to the 'Access channel' application-level permission.
claims.docFact: ContentTab.cs: All mutating operations require [PageCommand(Permission=VIEW)] + CheckAclPermission(UPDATE)

claims.claim: Page permission — one or more page permissions (Read, Update, Create, or Delete) for the target page or its parent.
claims.location: Permission requirements for page operations section (added)
claims.evidence: Confirmed across all operation source files: publish checks UPDATE (ContentTab.cs:200), create checks CREATE on parent (CreateWebPage.cs:133), delete checks DELETE (WebPagesApplication.cs:528), unpublish checks UPDATE (ContentTab.cs:313). All implicitly require Read via WebPageAclPermissionEvaluator double-check.

claims.claim: Workflow step role (if applicable) — if the page is under a workflow, the user must have a role assigned to the current workflow step or a Full Control role on the workflow.
claims.location: Permission requirements for page operations section (added)
claims.evidence: WorkflowStepRoleArbiter.cs lines 81-113: `CanWorkWithContentItemInStepInternal()` checks (1) IsDraft → always true (line 89-92), (2) HasFullControl → checks Full Control roles (line 94-98), (3) checks step role assignments (line 111-113). WebPageCommandManagerBase.cs line 554,601,648: calls `CanWorkWithContentItemInCurrentStep` for workflow-gated operations.
claims.docFact: WorkflowStepRoleArbiter.cs: Draft step always allowed, Full Control bypass, step role assignment

claims.claim: To publish a page, users need Read and Update for the page they want to publish.
claims.location: Page permissions for publishing pages section (added)
claims.evidence: ContentTab.cs line 200: `await CheckAclPermission(WebPageIdentifier.WebPageItemID, WebPageAclPermissions.UPDATE, ...)` — checks UPDATE. WebPageAclPermissionEvaluator.cs line 123 implicitly requires Read because UPDATE is not in IndividuallyCheckedPermissions. This matches permissionSummary.publish: 'READ + UPDATE on the page'.
claims.docFact: permissionSummary.publish: VIEW + Read + Update ACL. Under workflow, direct publish blocked

claims.claim: When publishing a newly created page (selecting the publish option during page creation), users need Read and Create on the parent page where the new page is being created.
claims.location: Page permissions for publishing pages section (added)
claims.evidence: CreateWebPage.cs lines 193-198: `[PageCommand(CommandName = WebPageActionConstants.PUBLISH, Permission = SystemPermissions.VIEW)]` followed by `await CheckAclPermission(ParentWebPageIdentifier.WebPageItemID, WebPageAclPermissions.CREATE, ...)` — checks CREATE on the parent. Read is implicitly required via the evaluator. This matches permissionSummary.publishOnCreate: 'READ + CREATE on the PARENT page'.
claims.docFact: permissionSummary.publishOnCreate: VIEW + Read + Create ACL on PARENT page. No workflow check

claims.claim: If the page is under a workflow, users cannot publish the page directly. Instead, they must advance the page through the workflow steps.
claims.location: Page permissions for publishing pages section (added)
claims.evidence: WebPageCommandManagerBase.cs lines 576-579: `if (await IsUnderWorkflow(webPage)) { return ... .AddErrorMessage(localizationService.GetString('workflows.cascadepublish.pagevalidation.contentitemunderworkflow')); }` — direct publish is blocked when under workflow. Must use ChangeWorkflowStep instead.
claims.docFact: permissionSummary.publish: VIEW + Read + Update ACL. Under workflow, direct publish blocked

claims.claim: To work with a page in a workflow step, users need one of the following: A role assigned to the current workflow step, or a Full Control role on the workflow.
claims.location: Page permissions for publishing pages section (added)
claims.evidence: WorkflowStepRoleArbiter.cs lines 81-113: After Draft check (line 89-92) and Full Control check (line 94-98), falls through to step role check (line 111-113): `assignedStepRoles.Any(stepRole => user.IsInRole(stepRole?.RoleName))`.
claims.docFact: WorkflowStepRoleArbiter.cs: Draft step always allowed, Full Control bypass, step role assignment

claims.claim: Pages in the Draft step are an exception — all users with the required page permissions can work with pages in the Draft step without a specific step role assignment.
claims.location: Page permissions for publishing pages section (added)
claims.evidence: WorkflowStepRoleArbiter.cs lines 89-92: `if (IsDraft(step)) { return true; }` — Draft step always returns true regardless of role assignment. IsDraft checks `step.ContentWorkflowStepType == ContentWorkflowStepType.SystemDraft` (line 125-128).
claims.docFact: WorkflowStepRoleArbiter.cs: Draft step always allowed, Full Control bypass, step role assignment

claims.claim: To create a new page or folder, users need Read and Create on the parent page under which they want to create the new item.
claims.location: Page permissions for creating pages section (added)
claims.evidence: CreateWebPage.cs line 133: `await CheckAclPermission(ParentWebPageIdentifier.WebPageItemID, WebPageAclPermissions.CREATE);` — checks CREATE on the parent. Line 186: repeated for the Create command. Read is implicitly required via WebPageAclPermissionEvaluator double-check. This matches permissionSummary.createPage: 'READ + CREATE on the PARENT page'.
claims.docFact: permissionSummary.createPage: VIEW + Read + Create ACL on PARENT page. Inherits from parent

claims.claim: Newly created pages inherit the page permissions of their parent.
claims.location: Page permissions for creating pages section (added)
claims.evidence: CreateWebPage.cs lines 271-278: When ParentWebPageItemID > 0, retrieves parent web page, gets `accessSettings` via `contentItemMemberRoleRetriever.GetAccessSettings(parentWebPage.ContentItemID, ...)`, and sets `arguments.AccessSettings = accessSettings` — new page inherits parent's access settings.
claims.docFact: permissionSummary.createPage: VIEW + Read + Create ACL on PARENT page. Inherits from parent

claims.claim: To delete a page, users need Read and Delete for the page they want to delete.
claims.location: Page permissions for deleting pages section (added)
claims.evidence: WebPagesApplication.cs line 528: `await CheckAclPermission(args.WebPageItemId, WebPageAclPermissions.DELETE, true, ...)` — checks DELETE. Read is implicitly required via WebPageAclPermissionEvaluator double-check. This matches permissionSummary.deletePage: 'READ + DELETE on the page'.
claims.docFact: permissionSummary.deletePage: VIEW + Read + Delete ACL. evaluateChildPages=true

claims.claim: When deleting a page that has child pages, the system also checks the Delete permission on all child pages that have broken the permission inheritance.
claims.location: Page permissions for deleting pages section (added)
claims.evidence: WebPagesApplication.cs line 528: `evaluateChildPages` parameter is `true`. WebPageAclPermissionEvaluator.cs lines 91-93: `if (hasPermission && evaluateChildPages) { hasPermission &= await CheckAclSubtreePermission(...); }`. Line 130 remark: 'For performance purposes, only web pages with broken inheritance are checked.' Line 146: InnerJoin with WebPageAclInfo filters to only pages with broken inheritance.
claims.docFact: permissionSummary.deletePage: VIEW + Read + Delete ACL. evaluateChildPages=true

claims.claim: To unpublish a page, users need Read and Update for the page they want to unpublish.
claims.location: Page permissions for unpublishing pages section (added)
claims.evidence: ContentTab.cs lines 310-313: `[PageCommand(CommandName = WebPageActionConstants.UNPUBLISH, Permission = SystemPermissions.VIEW)]` followed by `await CheckAclPermission(WebPageIdentifier.WebPageItemID, WebPageAclPermissions.UPDATE, ...)`. Read is implicitly required via WebPageAclPermissionEvaluator. This matches permissionSummary.unpublish: 'READ + UPDATE on the page'.
claims.docFact: permissionSummary.unpublish: VIEW + Read + Update ACL. NO workflow step role check

claims.claim: Unlike publishing, unpublishing does not check workflow step roles. Users with the required page permissions can unpublish a Published page regardless of its workflow configuration.
claims.location: Page permissions for unpublishing pages section (added)
claims.evidence: WebPageCommandManagerBase.cs lines 734-756 (`UnpublishInternal`): Only checks version status (InitialDraft/Draft at line 737, Unpublished at line 743, IsUnpublishScheduled at line 749). There is NO call to `CanWorkWithContentItemInCurrentStep` — unlike Save (line 554), ChangeWorkflowStep (line 601), and Discard (line 648) which all call it. This confirms no workflow step role check for unpublish.
claims.docFact: permissionSummary.unpublish: VIEW + Read + Update ACL. NO workflow step role check

researchRecommendationVerification.notes: Content uses a {% warning %} callout for the Read prerequisite rule, matching the recommendation to use Warning severity for pit-of-failure scenarios.

researchRecommendationVerification.topic: Explicit consequence documentation for missing prerequisites
researchRecommendationVerification.notes: Warning callout states 'the granted permission has no effect' and 'users cannot access page content in the administration' — explicitly documenting the consequences.

researchRecommendationVerification.topic: Contextual introduction before permission detail sections
researchRecommendationVerification.notes: The 'Permission requirements for page operations' section provides a multi-layer model introduction (application permission, page permission, workflow step role) before the per-operation sections.

researchRecommendationVerification.topic: Limit callouts to prevent notice fatigue
researchRecommendationVerification.notes: Only 1 new warning callout added (Read prerequisite). The existing tip in the channel admin section was updated in-place rather than adding a new callout. No callout proliferation.

researchRecommendationVerification.topic: Use consistent terminology for permission concepts
researchRecommendationVerification.notes: Content consistently uses 'page permission' (not 'ACL permission'), 'permission inheritance' (not 'access control inheritance'). Terminology is consistent throughout.

domainInsightVerification.title: Multi-layer parameter architectures require per-layer disambiguation
domainInsightVerification.notes: The 'Permission requirements for page operations' section explicitly disambiguates the three layers: application permission, page permission, and workflow step role. Each is described separately with its mechanism.
